content.problemStatement:
Chef recently took a course in linear algebra and learned about linear combinations of vectors. Therefore, in order to test his intelligence, Raj gave him a "fuzzy" problem to solve.

A sequence of integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã generates an integer KKK if it is possible to find a sequence of integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CMC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_MC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CM‚Äã such that C1‚ãÖB1+C2‚ãÖB2+‚Ä¶+CM‚ãÖBM=KC_1 \cdot B_1 + C_2 \cdot B_2 + \ldots + C_M \cdot B_M = KC1‚Äã‚ãÖB1‚Äã+C2‚Äã‚ãÖB2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+CM‚Äã‚ãÖBM‚Äã=K.

In this problem, Chef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and he should answer QQQ queries. In each query, he is given an integer KKK; the answer to this query is the number of pairs (l,r)(l, r)(l,r) such that 1‚â§l‚â§r‚â§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚â§l‚â§r‚â§N and the subsequence (Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,Ar)(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r)(Al‚Äã,Al+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äã) generates KKK.

Chef has no idea how to solve this problem ‚Äï can you help him find the answers to all queries?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains a single integer QQQ.
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains a single integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of contiguous subsequences that generate KKK.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The sequence (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) has three contiguous subsequences: (2)(2)(2), (4)(4)(4) and (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4).

In the first query, 111 cannot be generated by any subsequence.
In the second query, 222 is generated by subsequences (2)(2)(2) and (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4). For example, for the subsequence (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4), we can choose C1=1C_1 = 1C1‚Äã=1 and C2=0C_2 = 0C2‚Äã=0.
In the third query, 888 is generated by all three subsequences.
